meta.portfolioName: GoWell Energy Solutions — Zhang Family Trust Real Estate Portfolio
meta.preparedBy: Atlas Point by BPM — Asset Intelligence Platform

summary.narrative: Nine assets across five jurisdictions (Singapore, Houston TX, Johor MY, Hong Kong, Dubai) with aggregate assessed value of USD 31.3M and blended LTV of 28.0%, structured under a PropCo / OpCo separation framework — Zhang Family Trust holds equity through purpose-built PropCo entities while GoWell operating subsidiaries occupy properties as arm's-length related-party tenants. The Bulim Lane Singapore HQ generates USD 234K NOI at 6.73% cap rate under the GoWell Singapore related-party lease (SGD 4.00/sqft/mo); the Houston dormitory complex is master-leased to GoWell Americas LLC — both structures require full disclosure in the S-1/F-1 prospectus for GoWell's planned U.S. listing (Nasdaq / NYSE, H1 2027). CRITICAL: Regions Bank USD 3.65M matures 15 August 2026 — refinancing outreach must commence by June 2026 to avoid default and potential cross-default on GoWell corporate facilities.
summary.keyMessage: Portfolio AUM USD 31.3M · Zhang Family Net Equity USD 21.9M · Blended LTV 28.0% · CRITICAL: Regions Bank USD 3.65M matures 15 Aug 2026 — engage lenders by June 2026 · PropCo/OpCo separation requires full SEC / S-1 disclosure

riskSummary.topConcern: Zhang West Houston Housing LLC Regions Bank term loan (USD 3.65M, secured on the Houston dormitory complex) matures 15 August 2026 — the maturity wall compounds with a seller note (USD 450K) maturing February 2027. With Energy Corridor vacancy elevated and the GoWell Americas LLC master lease priced below market ($350/unit vs. $950 market), lenders may require a higher DSCR or equity injection in refinancing. Process must begin no later than June 2026 to avoid default, which could trigger cross-default clauses on GoWell corporate facilities and delay the U.S. public listing timeline.
